name: Build a CLI tool from scratch
task: Create a Python CLI tool at /tmp/eval_wc.py that counts lines, words, and characters in a file (like wc). It should accept a filename as argument and print: '{lines} {words} {chars} {filename}'. Handle the case where the file doesn't exist with a clear error message and exit code 1.

validation.script: echo -e 'hello world\nfoo bar baz' > /tmp/eval_wc_test.txt && OUTPUT=$(python3 /tmp/eval_wc.py /tmp/eval_wc_test.txt) && echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q '2' && echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q '5' && python3 /tmp/eval_wc.py /nonexistent 2>&1; test $? -eq 1 && rm -f /tmp/eval_wc_test.txt && echo 'OK'
